verses.text: Elders come to inquire, but God says they 'have set up their idols in their heart'; should He be inquired of by such? Secret idolatry. God will not be sought by those who cling to other gods within.
Elders come to inquire, but God says they 'have set up their idols in their heart'; should He be inquired of by such? Secret idolatry. God will not be sought by those who cling to other gods within.
Elders come to inquire, but God says they 'have set up their idols in their heart'; should He be inquired of by such? Secret idolatry. God will not be sought by those who cling to other gods within.
He who sets up idols in his heart and yet comes to a prophet, 'I the LORD will answer him... according to... his idols.' A solemn warning. God lets the hypocrite reap the delusion he has chosen.
He who sets up idols in his heart and yet comes to a prophet, 'I the LORD will answer him... according to... his idols.' A solemn warning. God lets the hypocrite reap the delusion he has chosen.

Repent and turn from your idols; against the man who comes to inquire while idolatrous God will set His face. Repentance is the only way. There is no fellowship with God while the heart still bows to idols.

Repent and turn from your idols; against the man who comes to inquire while idolatrous God will set His face. Repentance is the only way. There is no fellowship with God while the heart still bows to idols.

Repent and turn from your idols; against the man who comes to inquire while idolatrous God will set His face. Repentance is the only way. There is no fellowship with God while the heart still bows to idols.

If a prophet is deceived into answering such a man, both bear their iniquity, 'that the house of Israel may go no more astray.' Mutual guilt. God purges both false seeker and false guide for His people's good.

If a prophet is deceived into answering such a man, both bear their iniquity, 'that the house of Israel may go no more astray.' Mutual guilt. God purges both false seeker and false guide for His people's good.

If a prophet is deceived into answering such a man, both bear their iniquity, 'that the house of Israel may go no more astray.' Mutual guilt. God purges both false seeker and false guide for His people's good.
When a land sins and God sends famine, though Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, they would 'deliver but their own souls.' No borrowed righteousness. Each stands before God on his own.
When a land sins and God sends famine, though Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, they would 'deliver but their own souls.' No borrowed righteousness. Each stands before God on his own.
When a land sins and God sends famine, though Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, they would 'deliver but their own souls.' No borrowed righteousness. Each stands before God on his own.
Were these three present when wild beasts ravaged the land, they could not save even sons or daughters, only themselves. Personal faith. The godliest cannot transfer their standing to others.
Were these three present when wild beasts ravaged the land, they could not save even sons or daughters, only themselves. Personal faith. The godliest cannot transfer their standing to others.
So with sword and plague: Noah, Daniel, and Job 'shall deliver neither son nor daughter,' but their own souls by their righteousness. The point is pressed. Salvation is intensely personal before God.
So with sword and plague: Noah, Daniel, and Job 'shall deliver neither son nor daughter,' but their own souls by their righteousness. The point is pressed. Salvation is intensely personal before God.
So with sword and plague: Noah, Daniel, and Job 'shall deliver neither son nor daughter,' but their own souls by their righteousness. The point is pressed. Salvation is intensely personal before God.
So with sword and plague: Noah, Daniel, and Job 'shall deliver neither son nor daughter,' but their own souls by their righteousness. The point is pressed. Salvation is intensely personal before God.

How much more when His four sore judgments fall on Jerusalem; yet a remnant shall be left, and 'ye shall be comforted concerning the evil.' Mercy remains. Even in severest judgment God preserves and consoles a remnant.
